gentzen_tree.explanation: 'intro hnp' is ¬-Introduction: assume ¬P and derive ⊥. Then 'exact hnp hp' applies the assumed ¬P to hp : P, producing the contradiction.

steps.label: Assume ¬P (unfold the outer negation)
steps.explanation: ¬¬P means ¬P → False. We use intro to assume ¬P, naming it hnp. Now we have both hp : P and hnp : ¬P — a contradiction waiting to happen.

strategy.rule: To prove ¬A, assume A and derive False

steps.label: Apply the contradiction
steps.explanation: hnp : ¬P is really hnp : P → False. Applying it to hp : P gives False. We have P and ¬P at the same time — contradiction!

strategy.rule: When you have h : A and hn : ¬A, apply hn to h for False
strategy.trigger: You have both a proposition and its negation
